title: git_pull lacks URL/scope re-validation, allowing remote-set tampering to redirect updates
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/plugins/git.rs:79-94, src/plugins/manager.rs:488-525
## Violation
git_pull is dispatched against any directory under ~/.claude/marketplaces/ with no verification that the .git/config remote URL still points at the originally-vetted endpoint, and no re-validation against the current PluginPolicy. Quoted lines:

    pub fn git_pull(dir: &Path) -> Result<(), PluginError> {
        let output = std::process::Command::new("git")
            .arg("pull")
            .current_dir(dir)
    ...
        if dir.join(".git").exists() {
            git_pull(&dir)?;
        }

An attacker that can write inside the .git/config of an installed marketplace (filesystem race, supply-chain compromise of a nested plugin, or social-engineered "/plugin marketplace add" of a malicious local dir whose .git/config is then mutated) can repoint origin to an attacker-controlled URL. The next `/plugin marketplace update` then silently pulls and executes arbitrary content, bypassing strict_known_marketplaces (policy::check_marketplace_allowed only runs at add time, never at pull time per manager.rs:332-372 vs. 488-525).
## Mandated refactor
Before invoking git_pull, read the remote URL (shell out to "git remote get-url origin" with the absolute git path, or use gix-config), run it through validate::validate_source_url, and re-run the derived MarketplaceSource through policy::check_marketplace_allowed using the live PluginPolicy. Persist the canonical add-time URL alongside the marketplace install (extend InstalledPlugins or add a sibling marketplaces_state.json) and refuse to pull when the live remote no longer equals the recorded one. Errors should surface as PluginError::PolicyRejected to mirror the add-time contract.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
